items.title: AI Research Is Getting Harder to Separate From Geopolitics
items.summary: A policy change announced by NeurIPS, the world’s leading AI research conference, drew widespread backlash from Chinese researchers this week and then was quickly reversed.

items.title: Here’s why some people choose cryonics to store their bodies and brains after death
items.summary: This week I reported on some rather unusual research that focuses on the brain of L. Stephen Coles. Coles was a gerontologist who died from pancreatic cancer in 2014. He had spent the latter part of his career specializing in human longevity. And before he died, he decided to have his brain preserve...
